label: Purchase of any type of ammunition restricted to age 18 and older
description: Individuals must be age 18 or older to purchase ammunition from any type of dealer. No exemption for parental consent.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Purchase of handgun ammunition restricted to age 21 and older
description: Individuals must be age 21 or older to purchase handgun ammunition from any type of dealer. No exemption for parental consent.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Background checks required for ammunition purchase
description: Purchasers of any type of ammunition must undergo a background check, either at the point of purchase or when obtaining a permit for ammunition purchase.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Vendor license required to sell ammunition
description: Law requires that ammunition vendors be licensed. This must apply to all vendors of handgun ammunition; it may or may not apply to vendors of long gun ammunition.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Permit required to purchase ammunition
description: All firearm ammunition may only be sold to individuals who have a valid license or permit for that ammunition. This may or may not include purchase of ammunition from private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Records of ammunition sales must be retained
description: Vendors must retain ammunition sales records. This must apply to sales records of all handgun ammunition; it may or may not apply to sales records of long gun ammunition.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Ban on sale of assault weapons beyond just assault pistols
description: Law bans the sale of both assault pistols and other assault weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Ban on sale of assault weapons which includes a list of banned weapons
description: Law bans the sale of assault weapons and includes in this ban a list of some of the banned weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Grandfathered weapons must be registered
description: Law bans the sale of assault weapons. Individuals who already own assault weapons included in this ban must register their weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Transfer of grandfathered weapons is prohibited
description: Law bans the sale of assault weapons. Individuals who already own assault weapons included in this ban are prohibited from selling or transferring their weapons to others.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Ban on sale large capacity magazines beyond just ammunition for pistols
description: Law bans the sale of both assault pistol ammunition and other large capacity magazines.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Possession of pre-owned large capacity magazines is prohibited
description: No purchase or possession of large capacity magazines is allowed. Individuals who already own large capacity magazines included in this ban may be required to turn them in.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Ban on sale of assault weapons using a one-feature definition
description: Law bans the sale of assault weapons, using a generic definition of assault weapons based on only one feature.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No magazines with a capacity of more than 10 rounds of ammunition may be sold
description: Law bans the sale of all magazines with a capacity of more than 10 rounds of ammunition.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Background checks required for all gun show firearm sales at point of purchase
description: Law requires background checks for all firearm sales at gun shows at point of purchase. This closes the gun show loophole for all firearm sales. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Background checks required for gun show handgun sales at point of purchase
description: Law requires background checks for handgun sales at gun shows at point of purchase. This closes the gun show loophole for handgun sales. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: State conducts separate background checks, beyond the federal NICS check, for all firearms
description: Law requires state to conduct its own background check, not just the NICS check, for all firearm sales or permit applications. If at the point of purchase, must apply to gun show sellers and licensed dealers and may also apply to all other private sellers; if at the point of permit application, must apply to all licensed dealers and private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: State conducts separate background checks, beyond the federal NICS check, for handguns
description: Law requires state to conduct its own background check, not just the NICS check, for handgun sales or permit applications. If at the point of purchase, must apply to gun show sellers and licensed dealers and may also apply to all other private sellers; if at the point of permit application, must apply to all licensed dealers and private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Universal background checks required at point of purchase for all firearms
description: Both licensed dealers and private sellers must conduct background checks at point of purchase for all firearms. This may or may not include exemptions for buyers who have already undergone a background check for a concealed carry permit or other licensing requirements. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Universal background check required at point of purchase for handguns
description: Both licensed dealers and private sellers must conduct background checks at point of purchase for handguns. This may or may not include exemptions for buyers who have already undergone a background check for a concealed carry permit or other licensing requirements. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Background checks conducted through permit requirement for all firearm sales (or universal background checks)
description: Individuals must undergo a background check to purchase any type of firearm, either at the point of purchase or through a license/permit application. This may or may not include exemptions for buyers who have already undergone a background check for a concealed carry permit or other licensing requirements. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Background checks conducted through permit requirement for all handgun sales (or universal background checks)
description: Individuals must undergo a background check to purchase handguns, either at the point of purchase or through a license/permit application. This may or may not include exemptions for buyers who have already undergone a background check for a concealed carry permit or other licensing requirements. Background checks must be explicitly required.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Purchase of long guns from licensed dealers and private sellers restricted to age 18 and older
description: You must be 18 to purchase a long gun, regardless of whether it is from a licensed dealer or private seller. No exemption for parental consent.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Purchase of handguns from licensed dealers and private sellers restricted to age 21 and older
description: You must be 21 to purchase a handgun, regardless of whether it is from a licensed dealer or private seller. No exemption for parental consent.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Purchase of long guns from licensed dealers and private sellers restricted to age 21 and older
description: You must be 21 to purchase a long gun, regardless of whether it is from a licensed dealer or private seller. No exemption for parental consent.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: De facto registration of firearms is in place because of a recordkeeping requirement for all gun sales
description: All purchased firearms are registered with the state because of a requirement that all gun sales involve the permanent or indefinite retention of a record including the manufacturer, make, model, and serial number of the firearm; or, there is registration of all firearms.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: De facto registration of handguns is in place because of a recordkeeping requirement for all handgun sales
description: All purchased handguns are registered with the state because of a requirement that all gun sales involve the permanent or indefinite retention of a record including the manufacturer, make, model, and serial number of the firearm; or, there is registration of all handguns.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Mandatory reporting of lost and stolen guns by firearm owners
description: Firearm owners are required to report any and all loss or theft of their firearms.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Buyers can purchase no more than one handgun per month with no or limited exceptions
description: Buyers can purchase no more than one handgun per month, even if they have a concealed carry permit. In order to bypass this restriction, the buyer must be able to demonstrate an extraordinary need for the additional handgun. This may or may not apply to purchases from private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: A license or permit is required to purchase all firearms
description: All firearms may only be sold to and possessed by individuals with a valid license or permit to possess or carry firearms. This may or may not include requiring a firearm safety certificate and must apply to both licensed dealers and private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: A license or permit is required to purchase handguns
description: Handguns may only be sold to and possessed by individuals with a valid license or permit to possess or carry handguns. This may or may not include requiring a firearm safety certificate and must apply to both licensed dealers and private sellers.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Gun owners must register their firearms with the state
description: Individuals must register all firearms with the state, including the manufacturer, make, model, and serial number. Registration records must be maintained permanently or indefinitely.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Gun owners must register their handguns with the state
description: Individuals must register their handguns with the state, including the manufacturer, make, model, and serial number. Registration records must be maintained permanently or indefinitely.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Waiting period is required on all firearm purchases from dealers
description: There is a mandatory minimum waiting period for purchase of any firearm from a dealer. No exemption for concealed carry or purchase permit holders.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Waiting period is required on all handgun purchases from dealers
description: There is a mandatory minimum waiting period for purchase of a handgun (or handguns and assault weapons) from a dealer. No exemption for concealed carry or purchase permit holders.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: All firearms in a household must be stored securely (locked away) at all times
description: All firearms in a household must be stored securely (locked away) at all times.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: "May issue" state
description: Law provides authorities with discretion in deciding whether to grant a concealed carry permit, or the law bans all concealed weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Permit required to carry concealed weapons
description: Law requires a permit in order for an individual to carry a concealed weapon, or the law bans all concealed weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Aggravated assault count
description: Reported aggravated assault count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Burglary count
description: Reported burglary count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Homicide count
description: Murder and nonnegligent manslaughter count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Homicide rate
description: Homicides per 100,000 population.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Larceny count
description: Reported larceny count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Motor vehicle theft count
description: Reported motor vehicle theft count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Property crime count
description: Total property crime count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Property crime rate
description: Property crimes per 100,000 population, derived from property crime counts and annual population.
source: Derived from crime counts and FRED / Census population

label: Rape count
description: Reported rape count in the state crime series.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Robbery count
description: Reported robbery count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Violent crime count
description: Total violent crime count.
source: OpenCrime / FBI Crime Data Explorer

label: Violent crime rate
description: Violent crimes per 100,000 population, derived from violent crime counts and annual population.
source: Derived from crime counts and FRED / Census population

label: Population
description: Annual resident population used to construct crime rates.
source: FRED / Census state population

label: State dealer license required for sale of all firearms
description: All firearm dealers are required to have a state license.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: State dealer license required for sale of handguns
description: All firearm dealers that sell handguns are required to have a state license.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Dealers are liable for damages resulting from illegal gun sales
description: Dealers are held liable for damages caused by a gun that they knowingly sold to a person who is either prohibited from purchasing a firearm or who intends to use that firearm illegally.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Ban on non-commercial dealers
description: Dealers must have a commercial place of business; they cannot operate out of a home, car, etc.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Mandatory reporting of stolen guns by all firearm dealers
description: Dealers must report any and all thefts of firearms from their businesses.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Median household income (nominal)
description: State annual median household income in current dollars. The modern panel uses ACS values; the long-run demographic panel uses SAIPE with interpolation for 1990-1992 and 1994.
source: ACS 1-year / Census SAIPE

label: Median household income (2024 dollars)
description: Median household income deflated to 2024 dollars using annual average CPI-U.
source: ACS 1-year / Census SAIPE / BLS CPI

label: Poverty rate
description: Share of persons below the poverty line. The modern panel uses ACS values; the long-run demographic panel uses SAIPE with interpolation for 1990-1992 and 1994.
source: ACS 1-year / Census SAIPE

label: Share age 15-24
description: Share of population age 15 to 24.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Share age 25-44
description: Share of population age 25 to 44.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Share bachelor's degree or higher
description: Share of the education universe with a bachelor's degree or more. The long-run demographic panel uses Census anchors in 1990, 2000, and 2008 with interpolation before 2008, then ACS actuals.
source: ACS 1-year / Census Statistical Abstract Table 229

label: Share non-Hispanic Black
description: Share of population that is non-Hispanic Black alone or its historical Census population-estimate equivalent.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Share Hispanic
description: Share of population that is Hispanic or Latino of any race.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Share male
description: Share of population that is male.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Share non-Hispanic White
description: Share of population that is non-Hispanic White alone or its historical Census population-estimate equivalent.
source: ACS 1-year / Census population estimates

label: Log real PCPI
description: Natural log of real per capita personal income in 2024 dollars.
source: Derived from pcpi_real_2024

label: Log population
description: Natural log of population.
source: Derived from population

label: State law automatically prohibits domestic violence-related restraining order (DVRO) subjects from possessing firearms
description: Subjects of a domestic-violence related restraining order (DVRO) are not allowed to possess firearms. The law must apply to possession, and not just purchase of firearms. Application to handguns only is acceptable. The law must apply beyond family members, unless family members are defined to include ex-spouses and cohabitating partners.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: Law enforcement officials are required to remove firearms from people subject to a domestic violence-related restraining order
description: Law requires law enforcement officials to remove firearms from people subject to a domestic violence-related restraining order.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: State law requires DVRO subjects to surrender their firearms
description: Subjects of a DVRO are not allowed to possess firearms and are required to surrender the firearms that they already possess.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: Ex parte (temporary) DVRO subjects are automatically prohibited from possessing firearms
description: Subjects of a temporary (ex parte) restraining order are not allowed to possess firearms.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: State law requires ex parte DVRO subjects to surrender their firearms
description: Law requires subjects of an ex parte DVRO to turn in their firearms.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: All firearms must be removed from the scene of a domestic violence incident
description: Law enforcement officials must remove all firearms from the scene of a domestic violence incident. Firearms that can be removed cannot be restricted to only firearms used in the incident, firearms threatened in the incident, or firearms brandished in the incident.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: State law requires law enforcement to remove firearms from the scene of a domestic violence incident
description: Law enforcement officials are required to remove at least some firearms from the scene of a domestic violence incident.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: People convicted of a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence against a spouse, ex-spouse, or cohabitating partner are prohibited from possessing firearms
description: Law prohibits firearm possession by people convicted of a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence if the victim is a spouse, ex-spouse, or cohabitating partner. F10 The law must apply beyond family members, unless family members are defined to include ex-spouses and cohabitating partners.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: State law allows law enforcement to remove firearms from MCDV offenders
description: Law enforcement officials are authorized to remove firearms from MCDV offenders.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: State law requires law enforcement officers to remove firearms from MCDV offenders
description: Law enforcement officials are required to remove firearms from MCDV offenders.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: People convicted of a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence against a spouse, ex-spouse, or cohabitating partner are required to surrender their firearms
description: Law prohibits firearm possession by people convicted of a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence and also requires them to turn in the firearms they already have.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: A stalking conviction is prohibitive for firearm possession
description: Law prohibits firearm possession by people who have been convicted of a stalking offense.
source: Coded using a database created by Everytown for Gun Safety and Legal Science, LLC (http://everytownresearch.org/gunlawnavigator). Shared in accordance with the Creative Commons Attribution-4.0 International License (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/), which is incorporated herein by this reference (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/legalcode). No changes were made to the original coding, but the data were adapted for use in this database.

label: Per capita personal income (nominal)
description: Annual per capita personal income in current dollars.
source: FRED / BEA

label: Per capita personal income (2024 dollars)
description: Per capita personal income deflated to 2024 dollars using annual average CPI-U.
source: FRED / BEA / BLS CPI

label: Annual average unemployment rate
description: Annual average of the monthly state unemployment rate.
source: FRED / BLS

label: NICS handgun checks
description: Annual NICS handgun checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS long-gun checks
description: Annual NICS long-gun checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS multiple checks
description: Annual NICS multiple-firearm transaction checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS other checks
description: Annual NICS 'other' checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS permit checks
description: Annual NICS permit checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS permit rechecks
description: Annual NICS permit rechecks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS total checks
description: Total annual NICS firearm background checks.
source: Data Liberation Project / FBI NICS

label: NICS total checks per 100,000
description: Annual NICS total checks per 100,000 population.
source: Derived from NICS total checks and population

label: ACS dataset used
description: Mostly ACS 1-year; 2020 uses ACS 5-year because the 2020 ACS 1-year release was not produced.
source: ACS API

label: No possession of long guns until age 18
description: You must be 18 to  possess a long gun. No exemption for parental consent. Exclusions for adult-supervised hunting, sporting, or training activities are OK. Exception for possession on private premises NOT OK unless minor required to be under adult supervision.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No possession of handguns until age 21
description: You must be 21 to possess a handgun. No exemption for parental consent. Exclusions for adult-supervised hunting, sporting, or training activities are OK. Exception for possession on private premises NOT OK unless minor required to be under adult supervision.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No possession of long guns until age 21
description: You must be 21 to possess a long gun. No exemption for parental consent. Exclusions for adult-supervised hunting, sporting, or training activities are OK. Exception for possession on private premises NOT OK unless minor required to be under adult supervision.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No gun carrying allowed on college campuses except for concealed weapon permittees
description: Law bans gun carrying on college campuses, but concealed carry weapons may be allowed. No exemption for authorized faculty or staff, except security staff. Must include both public and private colleges.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No gun carrying on college campuses, including concealed weapons permittees
description: Law bans gun carrying on college campuses, including concealed carry weapons. No exemption for authorized faculty or staff, except security staff. Must include both public and private colleges.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No gun carrying on elementary school property, including concealed weapons permittees
description: Law bans gun carrying in elementary schools and on their grounds, even for persons with a concealed carry permit. No exemption for authorized faculty or staff, except security staff (including school resource officers). Must include both public and private schools.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Family members or law enforcement officers can confiscate firearms from any person who is deemed by a judge to represent a threat to themselves or others
description: Law must allow both family members and law officers to initiate an immediate process to confiscate firearms from individuals deemed by a judge to represent a threat to themselves or others. Law must require the surrender and confiscation of firearms, including authorization for a search and confiscation of the individual's residence. Must allow for temporary removal of weapons pending a court hearing.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Law enforcement officers can confiscate firearms from any person who is deemed by a judge to represent a threat to themselves or others
description: Law allows law enforcement officers (but not necessarily family members) to initiate an immediate process to confiscate firearms from individuals deemed by a judge to represent a threat to themselves or others. Law must require the surrender and confiscation of firearms, including authorization for a search and confiscation of the individual's residence. Must allow for temporary removal of weapons pending a court hearing.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No open carry of handguns is allowed in public places
description: Law bans the open carry of handguns in all public places, even with a concealed carry or handgun carry permit. Exemptions for open carry in the home, on personal property, in vehicles, and for hunting, target shooting, and training are permissible.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No open carry of long guns is allowed in public places
description: Law bans the open carry of long guns in all public places, even with a concealed carry or other permit. Exemptions for open carry in the home, on personal property, in vehicles, and for hunting, target shooting, and training are permissible.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No open carry of handguns is allowed in public places unless the person has a concealed carry or handgun carry permit
description: Law bans the open carry of handguns in all public places without a permit. Exemptions for open carry in the home, on personal property, in vehicles, and for hunting, target shooting, and training are permissible.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No open carry of long guns is allowed in public places unless the person has a permit
description: Law bans the open carry of long guns in all public places without a permit. Exemptions for open carry in the home, on personal property, in vehicles, and for hunting, target shooting, and training are permissible.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: People are required to relinquish their firearms after they become prohibited from possessing them
description: Law requires that upon becoming prohibited from possessing a firearm, a person must relinquish all firearms in their possession. This must be a broad provision that covers most, if not all, categories of prohibited people. For example, if the law only applies to mental health prohibitors, it is not considered to satisfy the requirements for having this provision. The law must explicitly require the relinquishment of weapons, not merely prohibit the possession of weapons.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Firearm possession is prohibited for people who have committed a violent misdemeanor punishable by less than one year of imprisonment
description: Law prohibits firearm possession by people who have committed violent misdemeanors punishable by less than one year of imprisonment. Simple assault misdemeanors must be included. Does not count if there is an explicit exemption for crimes punishable by less than one year of imprisonment.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Handgun possession is prohibited for people who have committed a violent misdemeanor punishable by less than one year of imprisonment
description: Law prohibits handgun possession by people who have committed violent misdemeanors punishable by less than one year of imprisonment. Simple assault misdemeanors must be included. Does not count if there is an explicit exemption for crimes punishable by less than one year of imprisonment.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: Firearm possession is prohibited for people who have committed a violent misdemeanor punishable by more than one year of imprisonment
description: Law prohibits firearm possession by people who have committed violent misdemeanors punishable by more than one year of imprisonment.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.

label: No stand your ground law
description: Use of deadly force is not allowed to be a first resort in public. This is sometimes referred to as a "stand your ground" law. There is a duty to retreat. Does not count as shoot first law if it only applies when person is in a vehicle.
source: Coded by Michael Siegel, MD, MPH, Boston University School of Public Health, with funding from the Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Evidence for Action: Investigator-Initiated Research to Build a Culture of Health program (grant #73337), using data derived from the Thomson Reuters Westlaw state legislative database.
